paradigm: Modular monolith, package-by-layer within app/src/ (context/, lib/marmot/, components/, hooks/). Within lib/marmot/ the dominant seam is hexagonal — pure-function modules (handlers/, cancelInvitationImpl.ts, pollSync.ts, epochResolver.ts) have zero React or IDB imports; all side-effects are injected as deps. registerHandlers.ts is the composition root.

patterns_to_mirror.name: pollSync template
patterns_to_mirror.notes: leaveSync.ts exports LEAVE_INTENT_KIND = 13, LeaveIntentPayload type, serialiseLeaveIntent(payload): string, parseLeaveIntent(content): LeaveIntentPayload | null. parse validates typeof d.pubkey === 'string'. No persistence counterpart.

patterns_to_mirror.name: pollHandler RumorHandler factory
patterns_to_mirror.notes: leaveHandler.ts exports LeaveHandlerDeps { enqueueLeave: (groupId: string, pubkey: string) => void }, handleLeaveIntent(rumor, ctx, deps), createLeaveIntentHandler(deps): RumorHandler. Zero imports from app/src/context/.

patterns_to_mirror.name: buildDispatcher registration
patterns_to_mirror.notes: S2 adds enqueueLeave to HandlerDeps and appends createLeaveIntentHandler({ enqueueLeave: deps.enqueueLeave }) to handlers[]. S4 supplies the concrete closure from MarmotContext.tsx:648 inside subscribeNewGroups.

patterns_to_mirror.name: cancelInvitationImpl Remove+commit+metadata (single commit)
patterns_to_mirror.notes: CANONICAL: single mlsGroup.commit({ extraProposals: [...removeProposals, Proposals.proposeUpdateMetadata({ adminPubkeys: filteredAdmins })] }) — both proposals in the same array, NOT two separate awaits. Includes guard: leafIndexes.length === 0 → return { ok: true, raceDetected: true }. PROPOSAL_TYPE_REMOVE constant; getPubkeyLeafNodeIndexes(state, pubkey) for lookup.

patterns_to_mirror.name: InviteCancelledChatAnnouncement (closer LeaveChatAnnouncement template)
patterns_to_mirror.notes: LeaveChatAnnouncement is simpler (one actor). Props: memberDisplay: string. Gray sidebar (bg=gray.50, borderLeftColor=gray.400). data-testid='leave-chat-announcement'.

patterns_to_mirror.name: parseStructured union + dispatch
patterns_to_mirror.notes: Add `| { type: 'leave_intent'; pubkey: string }` to StructuredContent union; add a guard branch in parseStructured(). Coverage: parseStructured.test.ts.

patterns_to_mirror.name: ChatBox renderStructuredMessage switch
patterns_to_mirror.notes: Add branch for structured?.type === 'leave_intent' (gated on allowPollMessages like invite_cancelled). Resolve memberDisplay from profileMap[structured.pubkey] with truncateNpub fallback. Render <LeaveChatAnnouncement memberDisplay={memberDisplay} />.

modules_touched_by_epic.name: lib/marmot/registerHandlers.ts
modules_touched_by_epic.story: S2 (handler registration); S4 supplies enqueueLeave closure

modules_touched_by_epic.name: context/MarmotContext.tsx
modules_touched_by_epic.story: S3 (leaveGroup body), S4 (pendingRemovals + debounceTimers refs + enqueueLeave), S5 (auto-commit)

test_invocations.typecheck: cd app && npx tsc --noEmit (no make wrapper)

test_patterns.purpose: Handler unit test (vi.fn deps, vi.mock idb-keyval + marmot-ts)
Two-client MLS e2e (bootUser × 2, real strfry, deterministic keypairs)

implementation_constraints: Static export: query parameters only, no dynamic path segments. router.push('/groups') resolves to a real static /groups/index.html.
Multi-platform dev: invoke build/test via make. Platform stamps at app/node_modules/.platform_$(uname -s)-$(uname -m).

Pending-removals queue (Map<groupId, PendingRemoval[]>) and debounce timer map (Map<groupId, NodeJS.Timeout>) MUST be useRef, not useState — no re-renders on mutation. Clear timers in the existing groupSubsRef cleanup at MarmotContext.tsx:720-727.
getPubkeyLeafNodeIndexes returns number[] — could be multiple indexes per pubkey in degenerate cases. leafIndexes.length === 0 means 'already removed' (race) — drop from queue, skip commit.
adminPubkeys is read inline from mlsGroup.groupData?.adminPubkeys at each call site (no isAdmin selector). S5 follows the same pattern.
S5's timer callback should re-fetch mlsGroup via clientRef.current?.groups.get(groupId) rather than relying on the closed-over reference (the subscription effect may have torn down).
kind-13 send in S3 happens BEFORE the local-state purge — encrypted key material must still exist to encrypt the rumor.
Historical sync replays kind-13 messages on restart through the same handler path. The 5s debounce absorbs the resulting burst; no first-sync gate is required.
open_questions_for_planner: S5's timer callback needs the mlsGroup reference. Cancel-invitation uses re-fetch via clientRef. The subscription effect captures mlsGroup as a const inside the for-loop — a tear-down race is possible if a 5s timer fires after the effect re-runs. Adopt clientRef.current.groups.get(groupId) as the canonical pattern in S5.

sendRumorSafe lives module-scope inside MarmotContext.tsx (not exported). S3 (leaveGroup) is fine — it's already in MarmotContext. S5's auto-commit is also fine — same scope. If the auto-commit logic were extracted to a separate file we'd need to move sendRumorSafe; staying inside MarmotContext avoids that.
S7 (member-list refresh) may be verification-only if the existing onMembersChanged callback at MarmotContext.tsx:599-609 already fires on local commits. Confirm during S7.
risks: If S5 implements the spec's earlier two-await wording instead of the now-canonical single-commit form, the ratchet tree and adminPubkeys will diverge for one epoch transition. Spec has been amended; planner/architect must read the post-amendment spec.

If the pendingRemovals or debounceTimers Maps are scoped inside the per-group subscription loop instead of at MarmotContext top-level useRef, each group will own an isolated queue and cross-group batching won't matter (it's per-group anyway), but cross-render persistence will break.

Historical sync replays kind-13 on every cold start. If two admins both start cold and both have queued leave intents in the relay history, both will fire the 5s debounce → both attempt to commit. EpochResolver covers this (deterministic fork resolution), but it does mean we get more wasted commits than in steady-state. Acceptable for v1.
